purpose: Defines a documentation/register/validation-only Multisensory Learning Engine for WellFit learning-through-movement concepts across visual, audio, movement, story, quiz, social, environment, optional AR, reflection, and AI Buddy explanation formats without enabling runtime learning personalization, protected-data tracking, reward authority, or mission-completion authority.

learningPrinciples.description: Movement can reinforce concepts through safe, optional, age-appropriate physical prompts that remain educational and non-authorizing.
Concepts may be paired with gestures, posture, rhythm, and spatial metaphors so learners can connect ideas to body-aware experiences without health inference.
Learning should feel exploratory and playful rather than pressured, shaming, addictive, or money-near.
Content must match reviewed age bands, reading levels, family context, and guardian expectations before runtime use.
Visual, audio, movement, story, quiz, social, environment, Buddy, and reflection cues can reinforce the same learning objective in complementary ways.
Learners can opt for safer or quieter alternatives and should not be forced into movement, social, camera, location, or AR modes.
Activities should provide clear, achievable steps and explanations that help learners feel capable without ranking or humiliation.
Family and social prompts should support encouragement and cooperation without pressure, comparison, or public embarrassment.
Learners should be invited to explain what they noticed, learned, tried, or would change, without sensitive profiling.
Every learning format needs accessible alternatives for mobility, sensory, language, attention, and device constraints.
The framework uses minimized planning metadata only and does not store raw protected data by default.
Learning outputs must not diagnose, treat, profile, or infer medical or psychological status.

allowed: planning-only movement prompts tied to an educational concept
low-pressure indoor or safe-place alternatives
opt-out and rest-friendly alternatives
reviewed age-appropriate physical intensity labels
forbidden: client-side movement proof as final mission-completion authority
movement signals as direct reward authority
overtraining, shame, streak pressure, night pressure, or unsafe route pressure
health inference, diagnosis, or biometric profiling

allowed: icons, diagrams, color cues, progress metaphors, and simple examples that reinforce concepts
high-contrast and text-supported alternatives
reviewed visual prompts that avoid protected or manipulative claims
forbidden: visual dark patterns, fear/shame cues, or purchase pressure
face, camera, image, or biometric processing without explicit review_required approval
using raw images or videos as direct reward or mission authority

allowed: optional narration, sound cues, rhythm, and recap prompts with captions or text fallback
volume-safe and quiet-mode alternatives
age-appropriate tone that supports understanding and reflection
forbidden: alarm-like pressure loops, shame audio, or compulsive notification patterns
voice analysis, diagnosis, or sensitive inference
audio signals as direct reward or mission-completion authority

allowed: short stories, character scenarios, worldbuilding, and analogies that connect movement to learning objectives
family-friendly and age-reviewed language
non-commercial narratives that do not hide sponsor or monetization pressure
forbidden: medical, psychological, financial, legal, token, wallet, NFT, betting, or payout advice
shame, FOMO, social humiliation, sleep-loss, or unsafe activity narratives
claims that imply runtime personalization or protected-data profiling is active

allowed: planning-only quizzes for comprehension, recall, self-explanation, and reflection
non-sensitive self-reported preferences with opt-out and no protected profiling
reviewed feedback that explains why an answer fits the learning objective
forbidden: quiz results as final reward or mission-completion authority
tests that infer health, psychological state, child risk, location, biometric status, or legal/compliance status
leaderboard pressure, humiliation, or money-near outcomes based on quiz results

requirements: AR/environment signals are planning-only in this framework.
Unity and PR #13 remain untouched.
Camera, location, and AR behavior remains review_required before any runtime use.
No unsafe routes, night pressure, street crossing pressure, or location-authority reward logic is allowed.
Environment tasks need safe fallback where possible, such as indoor, non-camera, non-location, or caregiver-supervised alternatives.
forbidden: runtime AR behavior activation
Unity file changes
raw camera, image, video, face, location, GPS, or biometric storage by default
environment/location signals as final reward or mission-completion authority

requirements: Buddy explanations must be age-appropriate.
Buddy must explain without pressure or shame.
Buddy must not give medical, psychological, financial, or legal advice.
Buddy can support learning, reflection, and motivation within Ethical Engagement boundaries.
Protected or sensitive cases remain review_required.
allowed: explain concepts in simple language
offer reflection prompts and safe alternatives
encourage autonomy, competence, relatedness, recovery, and accessibility
forbidden: diagnosis, treatment, therapy, finance, law, token, wallet, NFT, betting, payout, or medical advice
shame, manipulation, urgency pressure, or hidden monetization
claims that Buddy output authorizes rewards, mission completion, or protected-data decisions

allowed: cooperative family prompts and discussion questions
opt-in supportive sharing with safe fallback for solo mode
guardian-reviewed child/minor contexts before runtime use
forbidden: public embarrassment, comparison pressure, social ranking pressure, or coercive sharing
child/minor sensitive data collection without review and consent
social signals as final reward or mission-completion authority

requirements: Age bands, reading level, movement intensity, Buddy tone, quiz complexity, and family context require review before runtime implementation.
Child/minor contexts remain protected and review_required.
The framework does not infer age, maturity, health, or psychological status at runtime.
forbidden: age-inappropriate monetization or sponsor pressure
unsupervised risky outdoor, camera, location, or AR tasks for minors
medical or psychological interpretation of learner behavior

requirements: Provide non-movement alternatives where possible.
Provide captions or text fallback for audio.
Provide readable contrast and non-color-only visual cues.
Provide quiet, solo, indoor, low-intensity, and non-camera alternatives where possible.
Do not penalize users for using accessibility or safety fallbacks.
Health, child/minor, location, camera, face, biometric, motion, consent, and privacy data remain protected/review_required.
Raw images, videos, biometrics, health, and location data must not be stored by default.
Protected data may not be direct reward or mission-completion authority.
Data use requires minimization, consent, fallback, and review.
Runtime tracking, profiling, or personalization is not enabled by this framework.
Learning completion does not authorize rewards directly.
Quiz, movement, AR, Buddy, social, environment, visual, and audio signals are not final reward authority.
Rewards remain server/backend/review-required in future runtime.
No token, payout, wallet, NFT, betting, trading, staking, presale, purchase, or money-near mechanics are enabled.
Learning completion does not authorize mission completion directly.
Quiz, movement, AR, Buddy, social, environment, visual, and audio signals are not final mission-completion authority.
Mission completion remains server/backend/review-required in future runtime.
No token, payout, wallet, NFT, betting, trading, staking, presale, purchase, or money-near mechanics are enabled.

humanReviewRequiredFor: location, GPS, environment authority, or route-sensitive tasks
camera, image, video, face, biometric, or motion data
token, payout, wallet, NFT, betting, trading, payment, purchase, staking, presale, or money-near mechanics
medical, psychological, financial, legal, compliance, or protected claims
